title: solidago virgaurea subsp. litoralis
intro: Solidago virgaurea subsp. litoralis, also known as sea goldenrod, is a subspecies of the Solidago virgaurea plant, a flowering plant in the aster family native to Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes. The health benefits of sea goldenrod include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to support the immune system and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms. Sea goldenrod can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support respiratory and digestive health.
